stories.headline: Wasabi Protocol drained for $4.5M after deployer admin key compromise
stories.tldr: Attackers seized Wasabi's deployer key, swapped vault contracts to malicious versions, and drained roughly $4.5M across Ethereum and Base.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Admin-key takeovers keep beating audits — every perp DEX without timelocks or multisig deployers is one social-engineered laptop away from being the next Wasabi.
the_details: Compromised key granted attacker admin rights, then upgraded vaults — wWETH, sUSDC, wBITCON, wPEPE, Long Pool on Ethereum got drained.
Base deployment hit on the same key: sUSDC, wWETH, sBTC, sVIRTUAL, sAERO, sBRETT vaults all swept.
Brings April 2026 DeFi losses past $606M across 30+ incidents — worst hack month since the Bybit breach.

stories.headline: Lazarus owns April: $577M in two hits drives 76% of 2026 crypto theft
stories.tldr: TRM Labs pins 76% of 2026 hack losses on North Korea after $292M KelpDAO and $285M Drift exploits in a single month.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: State-actor concentration means defense isn't a protocol problem anymore — it's a geopolitical one, and Treasury's OFAC reach now follows every drained address.
the_details: Drift Protocol attack on April 1 cost $285M; KelpDAO breach on April 18 hit $292M — both attributed to Lazarus.
Cumulative DPRK theft since 2017 has topped $6 billion per TRM Labs' April 30 report.
Smaller April hits stack up: Resolv Labs $23M, Grinex $13.74M, Volo $3.5M, CoW Swap $1.2M, Wasabi $4.5M.

stories.headline: Banks lobby to slow GENIUS Act rollout as Agora sprints for stablecoin charter
stories.tldr: Major US banks are pressing for an extended comment window on stablecoin rulemaking while Agora races to be first chartered under the GENIUS Act.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The first non-bank to land a federal stablecoin charter resets the competitive map — banks know it, which is exactly why they want the clock slowed.
the_details: FDIC notice of proposed rulemaking sets requirements for FDIC-supervised payment stablecoin issuers under the GENIUS Act.
Bank trade groups requesting longer public comment periods before full implementation kicks in.
Agora is publicly pushing ahead with charter applications, betting on speed-to-market.

stories.headline: Bitcoin trading volume in freefall — pattern that rarely ends quietly
stories.tldr: BTC volume is collapsing into April's close even as price hovers at $76K, a setup that historically precedes sharp directional moves.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Thin liquidity plus geopolitical risk plus rising 30-year yields at 5% is a coiled spring — when volume returns, gap risk goes both ways.
the_details: BTC opened April 30 at $75,752, lowest since mid-month, before bouncing to $76,316 by 8:45am ET.
Spot BTC ETFs logged $137.77M outflow on April 29 — third consecutive day of bleeding.
Fear & Greed Index sits at 33; CoinDesk flags falling volume as historically pre-volatile signal.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms pressure Senate to mark up Clarity Act this session
stories.tldr: Coinbase, Ripple and over 100 firms signed a letter urging the Senate Banking Committee to advance the Clarity Act on market structure.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: SEC-CFTC joint guidance only goes so far — without statutory clarity, every token issuer still trades under enforcement risk that capital won't tolerate forever.
the_details: Letter targets Senate Banking Committee markup, citing March 17 SEC-CFTC joint interpretive release as foundation.
Industry argues delay risks pushing tokenized assets and stablecoin issuance offshore despite GENIUS Act passage.
Form 1099-DA cost-basis reporting now in effect for brokers, raising compliance stakes for unregulated venues.
